mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negatively correlated with month-over-month changes in RSAFS over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3%, it signals a tightening monetary policy that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3% over a 2-year period, or if the mean return is not negative with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) reinforce the signal of a robust expansion: employment gains boost disposable income, which translates into higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months when both PAYEMS and RSAFS rise month-over-month, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI YoY below 3%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects genuine economic growth that boosts corporate earnings and S&P 500 prices over a 60-day horizon. The signal reverses in high inflation due to cost pressures and tightening expectations.
failure_condition: If in months with CPI YoY < 3%, the rank correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to S&P 500 declines within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If a one-month increase in UNRATE does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p<0.05) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% signals a downturn in the housing market and broader economy, presaging weaker construction activity, employment, and consumer wealth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a HOUST decline trigger, the average 20-day SPX return is not negative and significantly different from zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive but inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising, the economy may be overheating, prompting expectations of monetary tightening that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the longer 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (GDPC1 growth * CPIAUCSL change) does not have a significant negative coefficient in a predictive regression of 60-day SPX returns (p>0.05) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), rising GDP signals potential inflationary pressure without labor slack, which may lead to tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 returns over the 60-day horizon. The mechanism is regime-dependent and flips sign in low unemployment environments.
failure_condition: If in months with UNRATE > 6%, the rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward 60-day returns is not negative (IC >= 0) over any 5-year window.
